bug_report.summary: Modern theme: Inspector section header row spacing/padding changes even when sections are collapsed (Node3D Inspector)
steps_to_reproduce: Launch the editor and set theme preset to Modern.
Select the root Node3D and look at the Inspector dock with the default collapsed sections (Transform, Visibility, Node, Process, etc.).
expected_behaviors: Only the spacing between a section header and its properties should increase when the section is expanded.
Collapsed section header rows should retain the same padding/spacing/layout as before (no added gaps/row height changes just from the spacing tweak).
actual_behaviors: Inspector layout changes are visible on the section header rows themselves (multiple headers/rows render differently), suggesting spacing/padding adjustments are affecting collapsed sections and/or header row layout rather than only the header-to-properties separation.

bug_report.summary: Pressing Escape closes the Editor Settings window (instead of just closing the open dropdown)
steps_to_reproduce: Launch Godot and open a project.
Open a dropdown inside Editor Settings (e.g., Color Preset), optionally scroll inside it.
expected_behaviors: Escape should close the currently open dropdown/popup menu, while keeping the Editor Settings window open.
actual_behaviors: Escape closes/dismisses the entire Editor Settings window, returning to the main editor view.
